mentions.note: Core auto business remains weak, but investors are focused on robotaxi and humanoid robot progress instead.
Preferred play for robotics exposure; stock flat recently but robotics potential significant; time for Tesla to deliver on automation promise.
Part of Magnificent 7; experiencing P/E compression like peers but company too strong to bet against long-term; stay the course.
Repeat: Tesla is robot company and autonomous driving play, not just car company. This quarter could define new narrative — if so, stock skyrockets from earnings announcement.

Doesn't trade on EV fundamentals (business in bad shape); trades on Musk storytelling. Need clarity on self-driving taxis and humanoid robots (promised end-2027 but history of timeline misses). Report Wednesday.
Reports Wednesday but trades on narrative not numbers. EV business weak. Musk credibility on self-driving taxis and humanoid robots (2027 launch) paramount — needs concrete progress updates.
Beat EPS and revenue Q4; 1.4B free cash flow impressive; robo-taxi/robot intros ahead of expectations driving after-hours strength.
Reframed as robotics/cyber/humanoid robot juggernaut, not just car company; Cramer buyer; give me five robots.
Robot company rhetoric overblown; XAI/SpaceX merger hype not compelling; hasn't made its move; less magnificent than General Motors; skeptical positioning.
Beat numbers but irrelevant; EV past, cyber cabs and robots the future; capex shocked, stock sold off 3%.
Core auto business deteriorating for 2 years; SpaceX IPO will eliminate scarcity value; expect stock to drift lower until robo-taxi or auto business turns around.
Rotation play; rallied 7.6% on recycled semiconductor news with no fundamental catalyst—pure rebalancing as Mag 7 peaks.
Market wants self-driving cars, robots, SpaceX IPO updates—not traditional auto metrics; narrative shift away from auto company label.
Cramer likes it; those who don't like Tesla probably short Intel too; robots and full self-driving are revolutionary.
Real growth engines are self-driving vehicles and robots, not cars; Musk potential SpaceX merger could unlock dual-class structure value.

Potential merger with SpaceX under Musk control ($1.5T+25% premium). Self-driving, battery, robot (humanoid) businesses; Starlink connectivity could enable distributed robot operations. Speculative but aligned with Musk's long-term vision.
Cramer expects SpaceX acquisition sooner rather than later; Mag 7 rotation from profitable free cash flow to profitability-unproven CapEx spending.
Magnificent 7 member; secular growth in electric vehicles; Cramer's charity owns six of seven Magnificent 7 (all but Tesla at time of statement).
